title: FG normal form
summary: Packages straight ambient data and defines the component matrix of the Fefferman–Graham normal-form metric in the t, base, and ρ directions.

mathematicalContent.heading: Normal-form metric
mathematicalContent.body: Relative to a chosen representative g in the conformal class, a straight ambient metric is written using a one-parameter family g_ρ with g₀ = g. In the ordered directions (0,i,∞) = (t,xⁱ,ρ), the file defines the corresponding component matrix.

mathematicalContent.heading: Component model
mathematicalContent.body: NormalMetricComponents stores only the numbers t, ρ, and gᵢⱼ at one formal point. ambientMetricComp then returns the displayed block components. This is a component definition, not yet a tensor assembled on a smooth ambient manifold.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Normal ambient data currently consist of the previously defined StraightMetricData: a base scale and a family ρ ↦ g_ρ compatible at ρ = 0.
This map wraps the stored normal ambient data as the formal StraightForm used by the ambient layer.
This accessor returns the chosen scale contained in A.
This accessor returns the complete one-parameter family of concrete metric fields.
correspondence.leanType: def gRho (A : NormalAmbientData Conf) : ℝ → Conf.MetricField

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: At ρ = 0, the concrete metric g₀ represents the conformal ray selected by the base scale at every point.
correspondence.leanType: theorem compat0 (A : NormalAmbientData Conf) (x : M) : Conf.toFiber (A.gRho 0 x) = A.baseScale.metric x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: At one formal normal-coordinate point, this structure stores the values of t, ρ, and the base block gᵢⱼ.
This function defines every component of the formal normal-form metric in the ordered 0, base, and ∞ directions.
correspondence.leanType: def ambientMetricComp (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) : AmbientIndex ι → AmbientIndex ι → ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The scale–scale component is twice the transverse coordinate.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_zero_zero (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero AmbientIndex.zero = 2 * C.rho

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The scale direction is orthogonal to each base direction in normal form.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_zero_base (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (i : ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero (AmbientIndex.base i) = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The transposed base–scale component also vanishes.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_base_zero (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (i : ι) : ambientMetricComp C (AmbientIndex.base i) AmbientIndex.zero = 0
theorem ambientMetricComp_zero_inf (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero AmbientIndex.inf = C.t

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The transposed transverse–scale component equals t.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_inf_zero (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf AmbientIndex.zero = C.t

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The tangential block of the ambient metric is t² times the slice metric g_ρ.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_base_base (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (i j : ι) : ambientMetricComp C (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = C.t ^ 2 * C.g i j
theorem ambientMetricComp_base_inf (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (i : ι) : ambientMetricComp C (AmbientIndex.base i) AmbientIndex.inf = 0
theorem ambientMetricComp_inf_base (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (i : ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf (AmbientIndex.base i) = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The transverse direction is null in this coordinate expression.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_inf_inf (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) : ambientMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf AmbientIndex.inf = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: If the base block gᵢⱼ is symmetric, then the complete ambient component matrix is symmetric.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientMetricComp_symm (C : NormalMetricComponents ι) (hg : ∀ i j : ι, C.g i j = C.g j i) (I J : AmbientIndex ι) : ambientMetricComp C I J = ambientMetricComp C J I

references.locator: Chapter 3, straight ambient metrics in normal form
references.note: Source for the normal-form expression and its block components.

limitations: This module defines a pointwise component model. It does not construct a smooth ambient metric tensor, prove nondegeneracy or signature, or derive the component matrix from a geometric normal-form theorem.
